A historic milestone in replica watches: successful development of the "Perpetual Calendar," one of the three major complications in white gold, rose gold, and diamond luxury watches! The Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Perpetual Calendar automatic mechanical replica maintains the original diameter without altering the case size, fully replicating all original functions. (Uses imported Seiko NH base movement modified for multifunction.)

Functions:
- Perpetual Calendar: Year display at the dot position runs normally (-). Adjustable via hidden case button, with reversible adjustment—better than the original, which requires costly repairs if adjusted incorrectly.
- Date, Month, Weekday (Annual Calendar): All indicators at dot positions run normally and can be manually adjusted (date via second crown position bidirectional adjustment; month via hidden button; weekday via pointer adjustment).
- Warning Window: Fan-shaped window above central pinion shows white when year adjustment is allowed, blue during forbidden periods to protect movement longevity—same as original.
- Moonphase: Dot-position moonphase window synchronized with original, completing one cycle per lunar month.
- Time: Hours, minutes, seconds run and adjust as a standard watch.
- Dimensions: mm × mm.

Note: The quick date adjustment in the crown’s middle position has a forbidden zone based on the date change at night—two hours before and three hours after midnight. Best to adjust date around noon.

Known issues:
- Thickness: Original is mm; replica is mm, slightly thicker but visually negligible and does not affect wear.
- Movement structure: Differences due to base movement variation. Downside: lower structural similarity. Upside: improved year adjustment with reversible cycling, unlike the original.
